# Smtp Send Tools

## Overview

gSage AI — Send Email tool (requires human approval).

## Tools in this group

| Tool | Summary | Permissions | Requires approval | Core |
| --- | --- | --- | :---: | :---: |
| `send_email` | Send an email via the configured SMTP server with optional attachments (requires human approval). | `email:send` | ✓ | — |
| `send_email_direct` | Send an email immediately, without human approval.  Restricted to the allowlist regex and/or the current user's own addresses. | `email:send_direct` | — | — |

## Configuration

> The JSON below goes into the **configtool model** in the web UI (per organisation; can also be scoped to a department).

> **Sensitive fields** (api keys / passwords / tokens) are *omitted* from the JSON below — set them via environment variables (see the _Environment variables_ table) or directly in the encrypted DB row from the web UI.

### Empty configtool template (required fields only)

```json
{}
```

### Example configtool model

```json
{
  "allowed_recipients_regex": [],
  "smtp_default_format": "html",
  "smtp_from_email": "<value>",
  "smtp_from_name": "<value>",
  "smtp_host": "<value>",
  "smtp_password": "<value>",
  "smtp_port": 0,
  "smtp_use_tls": false,
  "smtp_username": "<value>",
  "smtp_validate_certs": false
}
```

### Field reference

| Field | Type | Required | Sensitive | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | :---: | :---: | --- | --- |
| `allowed_recipients_regex` | `array` | — | — | — | Optional allowlist of regex patterns.  A recipient is accepted when it matches at least one pattern or when it is one of the current user's own addresses.  When empty, the behaviour depends on the tool variant: ``send_email`` accepts any recipient (approval already gates the action); ``send_email_direct`` restricts delivery to the current user's own addresses. |
| `smtp_default_format` | `string` | — | — | — | Default body format (overrides SMTP_DEFAULT_FORMAT). |
| `smtp_from_email` | `string` | — | — | — | Sender address in the From header (overrides SMTP_FROM_EMAIL). |
| `smtp_from_name` | `string` | — | — | — | Sender display name in the From header (overrides SMTP_FROM_NAME). |
| `smtp_host` | `string` | — | — | — | SMTP server hostname (overrides SMTP_HOST). |
| `smtp_password` | `string` | — | — | — | SMTP authentication password (overrides SMTP_PASSWORD). |
| `smtp_port` | `integer` | — | — | — | SMTP port (overrides SMTP_PORT). |
| `smtp_use_tls` | `boolean` | — | — | — | Use STARTTLS for the SMTP connection (overrides SMTP_USE_TLS). |
| `smtp_username` | `string` | — | — | — | SMTP authentication username (overrides SMTP_USERNAME). |
| `smtp_validate_certs` | `boolean` | — | — | — | Validate TLS server certificate (default true).  Set to false only for internal relays using self-signed certificates. |

## Environment variables

The variables below are derived automatically from each tool's `config_schema`. Set them in your `.env` (or via your secrets backend) to seed defaults for every organisation. **Per-org DB config takes precedence over env vars.**

| Variable | Maps to field | Type | Default | Sensitive | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | :---: | --- |
| `TOOL_SMTP_SEND__ALLOWED_RECIPIENTS_REGEX` | `allowed_recipients_regex` | `array` | — | — | Optional allowlist of regex patterns.  A recipient is accepted when it matches at least one pattern or when it is one of the current user's own addresses.  When empty, the behaviour depends on the tool variant: ``send_email`` accepts any recipient (approval already gates the action); ``send_email_direct`` restricts delivery to the current user's own addresses. |
| `TOOL_SMTP_SEND__SMTP_DEFAULT_FORMAT` | `smtp_default_format` | `string` | — | — | Default body format (overrides SMTP_DEFAULT_FORMAT). |
| `TOOL_SMTP_SEND__SMTP_FROM_EMAIL` | `smtp_from_email` | `string` | — | — | Sender address in the From header (overrides SMTP_FROM_EMAIL). |
| `TOOL_SMTP_SEND__SMTP_FROM_NAME` | `smtp_from_name` | `string` | — | — | Sender display name in the From header (overrides SMTP_FROM_NAME). |
| `TOOL_SMTP_SEND__SMTP_HOST` | `smtp_host` | `string` | — | — | SMTP server hostname (overrides SMTP_HOST). |
| `TOOL_SMTP_SEND__SMTP_PASSWORD` | `smtp_password` | `string` | — | — | SMTP authentication password (overrides SMTP_PASSWORD). |
| `TOOL_SMTP_SEND__SMTP_PORT` | `smtp_port` | `integer` | — | — | SMTP port (overrides SMTP_PORT). |
| `TOOL_SMTP_SEND__SMTP_USE_TLS` | `smtp_use_tls` | `boolean` | — | — | Use STARTTLS for the SMTP connection (overrides SMTP_USE_TLS). |
| `TOOL_SMTP_SEND__SMTP_USERNAME` | `smtp_username` | `string` | — | — | SMTP authentication username (overrides SMTP_USERNAME). |
| `TOOL_SMTP_SEND__SMTP_VALIDATE_CERTS` | `smtp_validate_certs` | `boolean` | — | — | Validate TLS server certificate (default true).  Set to false only for internal relays using self-signed certificates. |

_Note: any field above can also be overridden per-tool by using the prefix `TOOL_<TOOL_NAME>__` instead of the shared `TOOL_<NAMESPACE>__` — useful when a single tool in the family needs a distinct value._

| `send_email_direct` | `email:send_direct` |
